message:
fix(remote): add Drop impl to RemoteHandle for automatic cancel on abandonment

Previously, dropping a RemoteHandle without calling close() or abort()
would silently leak the remote task — the origin node lost its reference
but the worker continued executing indefinitely, consuming resources.

This adds a Drop impl that:
- For Pending/Running handles: sends a CancelRequest to the worker via
  the retained runtime reference, using the handle's own LogicalClockHandle
  to stamp a fresh send event (no Cx required at drop time)
- For terminal states: calls clear_runtime_state to clean up tracking

Supporting changes:
- cx.rs: expose logical_clock_handle() so RemoteHandle can capture the
  clock at construction time
- RemoteHandle gains origin_node and sender_clock fields, set during
  spawn_remote()
- abort() extracted into abort_with() helper shared by both the explicit
  abort path and the Drop impl
- spawn_remote uses logical_tick() (advancing the clock) instead of
  logical_now() for the spawn envelope timestamp

Tests added:
- attached_remote_handle_close_requests_cancel_and_drains_terminal_result
- dropped_remote_handle_sends_cancel_request (verifies Drop behavior)

Lab harness fixes:
- handle_spawn_ack: reject late SpawnAckStatus::Rejected when handle
  already transitioned past Pending
- handle_terminal_result: take tx before updating state to prevent
  sending on an already-consumed channel
- New tests: late_rejected_spawn_ack_does_not_clobber_running_remote_handle,
  duplicate_terminal_result_does_not_overwrite_completed_state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
